title: Port reviews.* and feedback.* into the dispatcher; bee_reviews.mjs + bee_feedback.mjs become shims
action: Per DB1-DB3 and the du-1 mechanism: add registry entries + handlers for reviews create/show/list/record/status, the NESTED 'candidate add' (3-token, longest-prefix from du-1) AND the separate flat 'candidates' verb (bee_reviews.mjs:186-207 — two distinct verbs, both pinned), and feedback's verbs (incl. rank) against lib/reviews.mjs and lib/feedback.mjs; --file|--stdin payloads must keep working. Preserve pinned texts and exit codes (A10 preflight refusal writes nothing, corrupt-session fail-open list, etc. are covered by existing tests). Replace both helper bodies with the group shim. Copy to .bee/bin siblings. Extend bijection coverage.

truths: all existing test_lib reviews/feedback CLI checks pass unchanged through the shims
bee.mjs reviews candidate add resolves via longest-prefix and matches legacy behavior
prohibitions: no logic changes in lib/reviews.mjs or lib/feedback.mjs

trace.outcome: Unified dispatcher now owns all reviews.* (create/list/show/record/candidate add/candidates/status) and feedback.* (digest/count/collect/rank) verbs: registry entries + handlers reusing lib/reviews.mjs and lib/feedback.mjs, a reviews group-usage fallback that also handles the nested 'candidate' subcommand mismatch, a feedback group-usage fallback, and both bee_reviews.mjs/bee_feedback.mjs collapsed to thin shims over main(['reviews'/'feedback',...]). Also fixed test_bee_cli.mjs's DA5 bijection verb parser (non-weakening) to correctly handle bee_reviews.mjs's trailing '(review modes: ...)' annotation and its two-word 'candidate add' nested-verb spelling.
trace.verify_output: test_lib.mjs: 220 passed, 0 failed | test_bee_cli.mjs: 102 passed, 0 failed | test_onboard_bee.mjs: PASS - failures: 0, skipped: 1 | combined chained exit=0
